senses.definition: to start to organize oneself for better results and more efficiency

examples.x: He needs to get his act together if he wants to succeed in his career.
She always procrastinates, but it's time for her to get her act together and start meeting deadlines.
After multiple warnings, he finally got his act together and improved his performance.
They struggled initially, but eventually got their act together and delivered a successful project.
I hope she will get her act together and take her studies more seriously.
They have a lot of potential, but they need to get their act together to achieve their goals.

senses.definition: to regain control over one's emotions, particularly after a period of being upset or angry

examples.x: She pulls herself together before stepping onto the stage to deliver her speech.
He pulls himself together and focuses on the task at hand despite the distractions.
After the initial shock, they pulled themselves together and started planning for the future.
They will need to pull themselves together to overcome the challenges that lie ahead.
They will have pulled themselves together by the end of the project and delivered outstanding results.

senses.definition: to force oneself to stop behaving in an uncontrollable manner, particularly in face of a difficult or unexpected situation

examples.x: She needs to get a hold of herself and approach the situation with a clear mind.
They often remind themselves to get a hold of themselves when they feel overwhelmed.
After the initial shock, he managed to get a hold of himself and handle the crisis calmly.
Despite the chaos, she eventually got a hold of herself and made rational decisions.
In challenging times, it is important to remember to get a hold of ourselves and stay focused.
They will need to get a hold of themselves quickly to effectively address the upcoming challenges.
If you find yourself in a difficult situation, try to get a hold of yourself before taking any action.
She would have resolved the conflict better if she had managed to get a hold of herself in the heat of the moment.

senses.definition: to start to act more organized in order to achieve better results and become more skillful or efficient

examples.x: He is determined to get his shit together and make positive changes in his life.
After a period of struggle, they got their shit together and turned their life around.
If you want to succeed, you need to get your shit together and take control of your life.

senses.definition: to not say something, against one's wish, in order to avoid causing an argument or upsetting someone

examples.x: She often has to bite her tongue during meetings to avoid getting into arguments.
I bite my tongue when my parents give me unsolicited advice to maintain harmony in the family.
He regretted not biting his tongue during the heated argument; his harsh words caused irreparable damage.
Despite her strong disagreement, she bit her tongue to keep the peace during the negotiation.
In sensitive situations, it's important to know when to bite your tongue and choose diplomacy over confrontation.
If you want to avoid unnecessary conflict, sometimes it's better to bite your tongue and let the conversation move on.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡɛt hæv ˈɔːl ʌv wˈʌnz dˈʌks ˌɪnɐ ɹˈoʊ

senses.definition: to get everything in order for something that is about to happen or one is going to do, particularly in order to avoid mistakes

examples.x: She gets her ducks in a row by creating a detailed checklist before starting any project.
He always gets his ducks in a row before presenting his ideas to the team.
After weeks of preparation, they got their ducks in a row and successfully launched the new product.
She had gotten her ducks in a row before the meeting, ensuring all the necessary documents were ready.

senses.definition: to not say anything or immediately stop talking, particularly so as not to reveal a secret or make matters worse

examples.x: She zips her lip whenever she hears something confidential.
We always button our lips during important meetings.
They zip their lips to protect the privacy of their clients.
She buttoned her lip when asked about the surprise party.
We would have buttoned our lips if we knew it was a confidential matter.

senses.definition: (of a situation) completely under one's control

examples.x: She has the project in hand and is confidently leading the team.
They have the situation in hand and are implementing the necessary measures.
After months of preparation, they had the event in hand and it went smoothly.
She had the presentation in hand and delivered it flawlessly.
By tomorrow, he will have the report in hand and ready for submission.

senses.definition: no longer desiring something or feeling strong emotions about someone

examples.x: She is having a good cry to get her frustrations out of her system.
They are talking it out to get their grievances out of their systems.
After venting to her friends, she felt like she had gotten her anger out of her system.
He went on a wild adventure to get his wanderlust out of his system.
If he doesn't express his feelings openly, he'll never get them out of his system.
She would have felt better if she had let her frustrations out of her system earlier.

senses.definition: to control a situation and stop it from getting worse

examples.x: The fire department is working hard to keep a lid on the out-of-control bushfire before it spreads further.
By clamping down on misinformation, the government had just barely kept a lid on public hysteria before a vaccine was found.
In the aftermath of the disaster, response teams will have successfully kept a lid on looting and lawlessness until relief arrives.

senses.definition: to make an effort to demonstrate a better behavior and treat others better

examples.x: They tell him he needs to clean up his act at work if he wants to keep his job.
My boss said I had to clean up my act after being late one too many times.
She warns that if he doesn't clean up his act soon, there will be serious consequences.
I've really cleaned up my act since meeting my girlfriend and getting serious about our relationship.
By the end of high school, she had finally cleaned up her act and graduated with good grades.
They will have cleaned up their act by the time the big inspection rolls around next month.
She would clean up her act immediately if she wants to keep this high-paying job.

senses.definition: one's face when it is devoid of any signs of amusement, interest, or even sadness

examples.x: I'm trying to keep a straight face while telling him the silly story.
I couldn't keep a straight face when remembering how she fell in the mud.
I'm forcing a straight face while she recounts the silly mishap.
She'll struggle holding a straight face when I retell that ridiculous story.
She would manage a straight face if it were anything else, not that anecdote.

senses.definition: to say things in order to settle a dispute or disagreement

examples.x: His ideas caused real dissension within the party at first, but he poured oil on troubled waters in last night's speech.

senses.definition: to calmly cope with something that is difficult or disturbing

examples.x: She takes criticism in stride, using it as an opportunity for growth.
Despite the setbacks, she took the failures in stride and learned valuable lessons from them.
By this time next month, he will have taken the criticism in stride and made significant improvements in his work.

senses.definition: to sensibly react to something and not treat it as if it is more serious, severe, or important than it actually is

examples.x: He struggled with keeping his expenses in proportion when he was in college.
The company has successfully kept its production costs in proportion with demand.
Tomorrow, we'll be discussing how to keep our project tasks in proportion.

senses.definition: to stay calm and sensible in a stressful or difficult situation

examples.x: She keeps a level head during high-stress meetings, calmly addressing challenges and finding effective solutions.
He always keeps a level head in emergencies, efficiently assessing the situation and taking appropriate actions.
Despite the chaos, she kept a level head throughout the crisis, guiding her team with clarity and composure.
They will keep a level head during the upcoming negotiations, staying composed and advocating for their interests.
If you want to succeed in high-pressure situations, you must keep a level head and approach challenges with a clear mind.

senses.definition: to get one's thoughts or feelings under control, particularly after experiencing something shocking or disturbing

examples.x: She collects her thoughts before responding, ensuring a clear and concise answer.
Despite the pressure, she collected her thoughts and delivered a well-thought-out presentation.
He collected his thoughts after the argument, reflecting on his words and seeking a resolution.
If you collect your thoughts before responding, you can express yourself more effectively.
She would have resolved the issue faster if she had collected her thoughts before taking action.

senses.definition: the willpower to overcome one's problems or control one's physical condition

examples.x: She practices mindfulness to harness the power of mind over matter, focusing her thoughts to alleviate physical discomfort.
He believes in the principle of mind over matter, pushing his body to new limits during intense workouts.
He had used mind over matter to overcome his fear of heights, conquering the challenge with sheer determination.
They will use mind over matter during their expedition, relying on mental resilience to endure harsh conditions.
Over the years, he has developed a strong belief in mind over matter, using mental techniques to overcome challenges and achieve success.

senses.definition: to stop behaving foolishly or recklessly and gain full control of one's emotions and thoughts

examples.x: After getting fired, John needs to take a walk to get a grip on himself before talking to his wife.
When Sarah found out she didn't get the promotion, she had to get a grip on herself to keep from crying at her desk.
Once the panic of failing her exam subsides, Jenny will get a grip on herself and make a plan to study harder for the retake.
Having dealt with anxiety attacks before, Kevin has learned strategies to help him get a grip on himself when he starts feeling one coming on.
By the time the meeting was over, Roger had fully got a grip on himself after momentarily losing his cool when criticized.
Monica will have got a grip on herself by tonight so she can evaluate job offers with a level head tomorrow.
If he takes some deep breaths, Brad should be able to get a grip on himself and drive himself safely home.

senses.definition: to stay calm and think clearly, especially in challenging or stressful situations

examples.x: She keeps her head during high-pressure negotiations, carefully weighing her options before making decisions.
He always keeps his head in challenging situations, maintaining a calm and focused demeanor.
They will keep their heads during the upcoming challenges, relying on their experience and expertise.
They have already kept their heads during multiple crises, demonstrating their ability to handle pressure.
